findings.message: The catalogue-driven mapping table names inputs that do not match the actual `TypeDefinitionKind` model: `Typestate` is not driven by `transitions_to`, `FreeFunction` is not `params`/`returns`, and `SecondaryAdapter` does not have a top-level `expected_methods` field. A generator following this table would read the wrong catalogue fields instead of `transitions`, `expected_params`/`expected_returns`/`expected_is_async`, and `implements` with nested method declarations.

The target filter says `A\B + A‚ą©B` means ‚Äúnew/changed types only‚ÄĚ, but in TDDD-02 this is simply the declared catalogue set `A`; TDDD-03 further allows `A‚ą©B` entries to be `action: reference`, i.e. unchanged context declarations. Set membership alone therefore cannot select only new/changed types, so the proposed generation scope would include reference-only existing types unless it also filters by `TypeAction`.
